title: Research to Production Playbook - Jayakrishna Konda
content:
How new research gets converted into production use-cases:

Step 1 - Identify applicable method: He selects methods that are directly mappable to business workflows, especially in NLP, document intelligence, and retrieval-heavy tasks.

Step 2 - Build with orchestration tooling: He uses LangChain/LangGraph/LlamaIndex and FAISS-based retrieval design to create multi-step, tool-enabled pipelines instead of single-prompt prototypes.

Step 3 - Validate with measurable evaluation: He applies metrics and evaluation frameworks (BLEU/ROUGE/BERTScore, A/B tests, human-in-the-loop checks, model validation routines) to avoid subjective quality claims.

Step 4 - Productionize with MLOps controls: He deploys with Docker/Kubernetes and CI/CD, integrates model/version governance (MLflow), and adds monitoring/drift checks and alerting.

Step 5 - Optimize for real operations: He iterates on latency, throughput, and cost (for example, reducing inference latency and lowering cloud spend), and hardens reliability in a multi-service production environment.

Representative outcomes from this playbook include reduced review effort, faster inference, and stable high-availability operations in both enterprise and self-hosted stacks.
